task: Set combobox value in non-standard hardware UI
user_message: set the policy dropdown to DROP on the ER605 ACL page
good_response: Route to it_agent. The ER605 uses custom combobox widgets — #policy is a hidden input, not a select element. Pattern: el.parentElement → .combobox-container → click .combobox-switch → click label.combobox-label with input[value='DROP']. Use Python requests.post for proper JS escaping.
bad_response: Route to code_review_agent. This is a CSS selector issue — try document.querySelector('#policy option[value=DROP]').selected = true.
good_plan: Inspect DOM to understand combobox structure
Use parentElement traversal to find container
Click switch to open dropdown
Click target label to select
Verify hidden input value changed
bad_plan: Try setting .value directly on hidden input
why_good_is_better: ER605 comboboxes are NOT standard HTML selects — option[value] doesn't exist
Setting .value on hidden input doesn't trigger the UI update
it_agent has hardware UI expertise, code_review doesn't interact with UIs
The parentElement traversal pattern works because the combobox is a wrapper around a hidden input

task: Debug 500 error from browser automation endpoint
user_message: the /evaluate endpoint returns 500 when I send JavaScript with quotes
good_response: Route to it_agent. The 500 is caused by curl shell escaping corrupting JS with mixed quotes. Switch to Python requests.post(url, json={'expression': js}) — json= handles escaping automatically. The browser-worker service itself is fine.
bad_response: Route to self_healer_agent. The evaluate endpoint is returning 500 errors, which means the service crashed and needs restarting.
good_plan: Identify that the error is request formatting, not service failure
Switch from curl to Python requests for proper JSON encoding
Verify the expression works with correct escaping
bad_plan: Add error handling to the evaluate endpoint
why_good_is_better: The service isn't broken — the request is malformed due to shell escaping
Restarting the pod would change nothing — same curl command would fail again
it_agent understands browser automation API interaction patterns
self_healer handles crashes, not request formatting issues

user_message: I submitted the ACL form but nothing happened — no error, no new row
good_response: Route to it_agent. The ER605 ACL form has a silent validation requirement: the time field MUST be set to 'Any' — it defaults to empty, not 'Any'. Also check that the rule name uses underscores only (no hyphens). Set all required fields and resubmit.
bad_response: Route to self_healer_agent. The form submission failed silently, which indicates a frontend bug that needs to be fixed.
good_plan: Check all form fields are set (especially time)
Verify name uses valid characters (underscores only)
Set time to 'Any' explicitly
Resubmit and verify
bad_plan: File a bug report about silent validation
Try to fix the ER605 firmware
why_good_is_better: Silent validation is a known ER605 UI behavior, not a bug
The time field defaults to empty — this is hardware-specific knowledge
it_agent knows these hardware quirks, self_healer doesn't interact with UIs
The fix is setting a missing field value, not healing a broken system

task: Process scanned document for training data
user_message: extract text from this PDF invoice so I can use it for training
good_response: Route to data_agent for the data pipeline, using document_ocr tool. GLM-OCR (0.9B model, localhost:5002) converts PDFs and images to clean Markdown. Use document_ocr to extract, then validate schema and format for training JSONL.
bad_response: Route to knowledge_agent. This is about reading a document, and knowledge_agent handles document search.
good_plan: Use document_ocr to extract Markdown from PDF
Validate extracted content quality
Transform into training data format
Append to appropriate JSONL file
bad_plan: Try to read the PDF as raw text
Skip OCR and paste the raw bytes
why_good_is_better: PDFs need OCR extraction before LLM can process them
data_agent handles ETL — extraction → transform → load
knowledge_agent searches existing vectorized docs, it doesn't extract new ones
GLM-OCR produces structured Markdown with tables and headings intact

task: Use OCR to read network diagram image
user_message: read the network topology diagram and tell me what VLANs are configured
good_response: Route to it_agent with document_ocr tool. Extract text from the diagram image using GLM-OCR, then interpret the network topology against known VLAN configuration. it_agent has both OCR access and network domain knowledge.
bad_response: Route to knowledge_agent. This is about reading a document image.
good_plan: Use document_ocr to extract text from image
Parse VLAN IDs, names, and subnet information
Cross-reference against known network_vlan_strategy
Report discrepancies if any
bad_plan: Try to read the image as a text file
Route to knowledge_agent without OCR capability
Describe the image without extracting data
why_good_is_better: it_agent has document_ocr tool AND network expertise
knowledge_agent doesn't have OCR access
Interpreting network diagrams requires infrastructure domain knowledge
The combined OCR + network knowledge produces an expert answer
